Title: A histone deacetylase complex regulates anthocyanin biosynthesis during normal plant growth and development and in response to jasmonate

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment involved in abiotic stress tolerance and defense against herbivores/pathogens
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petiole; shoot apical meristem region; seedling aerial tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin accumulation by contributing redundantly to PAP1 repression
Common_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis via chromatin deacetylation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant analysis; genetic complementation; qPCR; anthocyanin quantification
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SNL2; SNL3; SNL4; SNL5; HDC1; HDA19; VAL1; VAL2
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; methyl jasmonate treatment/withdrawal for pathway dynamics context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SNL1 acts partially redundantly with SNL2-SNL5 to inhibit anthocyanin biosynthesis under normal growth conditions. 2. Loss of multiple SNL genes strongly derepresses PAP1 and elevates anthocyanin accumulation. 3. Complementation with genomic SNL1 restores the phenotype, supporting a causal role in repression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing SNL function could increase anthocyanin content, but likely requires caution because SNL chromatin regulators affect multiple developmental processes.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic effects due to broad chromatin regulatory roles; higher-order mutants needed for strong phenotype
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as part of the SNL-HDA19 histone deacetylase complex rather than as a catalytic pathway enzyme.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment involved in abiotic stress tolerance and defense against herbivores/pathogens
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petiole; shoot apical meristem region; seedling aerial tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin accumulation by contributing redundantly to PAP1 repression
Common_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis via chromatin deacetylation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single/double/higher-order mutant analysis; qPCR; anthocyanin quantification; complementation
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SNL1; SNL3; SNL4; SNL5; HDC1; HDA19; VAL1; VAL2
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; methyl jasmonate context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. snl2 single mutants show moderately increased anthocyanin. 2. Combined loss with other SNLs, especially SNL1, enhances anthocyanin accumulation, showing partial redundancy. 3. SNL2 contributes to repression of PAP1-dependent anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate negative regulator for anthocyanin biofortification by derepression strategies, with pleiotropy risk.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad chromatin regulatory role suggests developmental side effects possible
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of the SNL-HDA19c scaffold module.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment involved in abiotic stress tolerance and defense against herbivores/pathogens
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petiole; shoot apical meristem region; seedling aerial tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin accumulation redundantly through PAP1 repression
Common_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis via chromatin deacetylation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single/double/higher-order mutant analysis; complementation; qPCR; anthocyanin quantification
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SNL1; SNL2; SNL4; SNL5; HDC1; HDA19; VAL1; VAL2
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; methyl jasmonate context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. snl3 mutants show moderate anthocyanin increase. 2. Combined mutations with other SNLs increase anthocyanin additively/redundantly. 3. Genomic SNL3 complementation restores repression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to elevate anthocyanins by weakening repression, preferably with targeted/tissue-specific strategies.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment involved in abiotic stress tolerance and defense against herbivores/pathogens
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petiole; shoot apical meristem region; seedling aerial tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly associates with PAP1 chromatin as part of a repressive deacetylase complex and suppresses anthocyanin accumulation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Higher-order mutant analysis; genetic complementation; ChIP-qPCR; co-IP; anthocyanin quantification
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; MeJA treatment and withdrawal
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SNL4 binds the PAP1 proximal promoter and 5'UTR. 2. Its enrichment on PAP1 requires VAL1/VAL2. 3. JA treatment transiently releases SNL4 from PAP1 chromatin, correlating with PAP1 activation and anthocyanin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A direct chromatin-level lever for tuning anthocyanin accumulation dynamics in response to stress signals.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely broad effects on many genes due to genome-wide chromatin recruitment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Studied with functional SNL4:HA transgene; one of the best-supported SNL subunits in this paper.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment involved in abiotic stress tolerance and defense against herbivores/pathogens
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petiole; shoot apical meristem region; seedling aerial tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin accumulation redundantly with SNL1-SNL4
Common_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis via chromatin deacetylation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Single and higher-order mutant analysis; complementation; anthocyanin quantification
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SNL1; SNL2; SNL3; SNL4; HDC1; HDA19; VAL1; VAL2
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; methyl jasmonate context
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. snl5 single mutants have moderately elevated anthocyanin. 2. Incorporation of snl5 into higher-order mutant backgrounds further elevates anthocyanin content. 3. Genomic SNL5 complements the phenotype.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Negative regulator that could be manipulated for anthocyanin enhancement, though redundancy means multi-gene intervention may be required.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Multi-gene redundancy and likely pleiotropy complicate deployment
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Partially redundant member of the SNL scaffold family.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment involved in abiotic stress tolerance and defense
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petiole; shoot apical meristem region; seedling aerial tissues
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses PAP1 expression and anthocyanin accumulation through histone deacetylation at PAP1 chromatin
Common_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Histone deacetylation-mediated transcriptional repression of anthocyanin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant; genomic complementation with HDA19:GFP; ChIP-qPCR; co-IP; anthocyanin quantification; pap1 epistasis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; MeJA treatment and withdrawal
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hda19 mutants show PAP1 derepression and elevated anthocyanin accumulation. 2. HDA19:GFP complements the mutant phenotype and is enriched on PAP1 chromatin. 3. JA reduces HDA19-complex occupancy indirectly through release of VAL1/SNL module; pap1 abolishes hda19 anthocyanin overaccumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: An effective negative regulator for anthocyanin enhancement, but broad HDAC functions make precise or conditional engineering preferable.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pleiotropic developmental phenotypes in hda19; likely regulates many genes outside anthocyanin pathway
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Extent of PAP1 derepression in hda19 is less than in snl quintuple or val1 val2, implying other HDAC partners may contribute.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment involved in abiotic stress tolerance and defense
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses PAP1 expression and anthocyanin accumulation as a component of SNL-HDA19c
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SNL1; SNL2; SNL3; SNL4; SNL5; HDA19
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of HDC1 strongly derepresses PAP1. 2. hdc1 seedlings accumulate more anthocyanins than WT. 3. These results support HDC1 as part of the functional SNL-HDC1-HDA19 repressor complex.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible engineering node to increase anthocyanins by relieving chromatin repression.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment promoting adaptation to abiotic stress and defense
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petiole; shoot apical meristem region; seedling aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin biosynthesis; transcriptional repression lowers anthocyanin, while derepression/activation increases anthocyanin
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates DFR and other structural genes in the anthocyanin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator of anthocyanin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; mutant epistasis (pap1 introduced into snl1/2/3/4/5 and hda19); promoter mutagenesis; ChIP-qPCR of upstream repressors; anthocyanin quantification
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: JAZ proteins; GL3; EGL3; TT8; TTG1/MBW complex components
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate/MeJA; chromatin repression by VAL1/VAL2-SNL-HDA19c; environmental stresses
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; MeJA treatment and withdrawal
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAP1 is strongly derepressed in snl1/2/3/4/5, hda19, and hdc1 mutants. 2. pap1 mutation abolishes anthocyanin overaccumulation in snl quintuple and hda19, establishing PAP1 as required for the phenotype. 3. JA transiently activates PAP1 transcription through release of the VAL1-JAZ-SNL-HDA19c repression module.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overproduction may disturb growth-defense balance; natural regulation prevents overaccumulation
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Histone deacetylation at PAP1 promoter/5'UTR by VAL1/VAL2-recruited SNL-HDA19c represses expression; JA induces increased histone acetylation after complex release.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Paper proposes a feedforward regulatory mechanism in JA-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although PAP1 is itself a regulator, it is also the central transcriptional target of the chromatin repression module identified in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment promoting stress adaptation and defense
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petiole; shoot apical meristem region; seedling aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses PAP1 transcription and anthocyanin biosynthesis under normal conditions; JA causes its release from PAP1 chromatin, enabling anthocyanin induction
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression via DNA binding and recruitment of histone deacetylase complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses PAP1 by binding RY motifs in the PAP1 proximal promoter and 5'UTR and recruiting SNL-HDA19c
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential recruiter of the repressive chromatin module controlling PAP1 and anthocyanin output during normal growth and JA response
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; yeast two-hybrid; co-IP; mutant analysis; promoter RY-box mutagenesis; anthocyanin quantification
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HDA19; SNL1; SNL4; JAZ1; JAZ3; JAZ4; JAZ9; VAL2
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate status through JAZ proteins; RY cis-elements at PAP1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal growth conditions; MeJA treatment and withdrawal
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VAL1 binds RY motifs in the PAP1 promoter/5'UTR. 2. VAL1 physically interacts with HDA19, SNL proteins, and JAZ proteins. 3. val1 val2 mutants show PAP1 derepression and increased anthocyanin; JA treatment transiently removes VAL1 from PAP1 chromatin.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad genome-wide repression of many JA-responsive and developmental genes may cause pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Functions by recruiting histone deacetylase complex to PAP1 chromatin for histone deacetylation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Central mechanistic regulator in the study.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment promoting stress adaptation and defense
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Petiole; shoot apical meristem region; seedling aerial tissues
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions redundantly with VAL1 to repress PAP1 and inhibit anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression via recruitment of SNL-HDA19c to PAP1 chromatin
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required redundantly with VAL1 for strong repression of PAP1 and low basal anthocyanin levels
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; ChIP dependency assay for SNL4 recruitment; anthocyanin quantification
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate context inferred via shared module with VAL1 and JAZs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VAL2 functions redundantly with VAL1 in repressing PAP1. 2. Loss of both VAL1 and VAL2 nearly abolishes SNL4 enrichment at PAP1. 3. val1 val2 double mutants accumulate more anthocyanin than WT or single mutants.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad chromatin silencing functions beyond anthocyanin pathway
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Participates in recruitment of histone deacetylation machinery to PAP1 chromatin.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is somewhat less direct than for VAL1 at PAP1, but val1 val2 genetics establish functional redundancy.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: JAZ/TIFY family; ZIM domain and Jas domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigment promoting stress adaptation and defense
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Represses anthocyanin biosynthesis by stabilizing VAL1 and HDA19 complex occupancy at PAP1; degradation upon JA accumulation relieves repression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein-protein interaction-mediated repression within jasmonate signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly represses PAP1 by stabilizing VAL1 and SNL-HDA19c binding to PAP1 chromatin
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key JA-responsive brake whose removal allows rapid PAP1 activation and anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pull-down; co-IP; JAZ1ΔJas gain-of-function line; ChIP-qPCR of VAL1 and HDA19 occupancy
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Jasmonate/MeJA causes JAZ degradation via canonical JA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ1 physically associates with VAL1. 2. Constitutively stable JAZ1ΔJas enhances VAL1 and HDA19 enrichment at PAP1 chromatin. 3. JAZ1-mediated stabilization of the repression complex contributes to suppression of PAP1 and anthocyanin biosynthesis in the absence of JA.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: JAZ proteins broadly regulate JA-mediated defense and growth; constitutive stabilization suppresses JA responses
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes JAZ proteins as a second regulatory layer beyond interference with MBW complex assembly.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to repression of PAP1/anthocyanin module through interaction with VAL1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect PAP1 repression inferred through VAL1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ3 C-terminal region interacts with VAL1 in yeast. 2. This supports the idea that multiple JAZ repressors can connect JA signaling to the VAL1-SNL-HDA19c module. 3. Functional redundancy likely obscures single-gene phenotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad JA signaling roles likely cause pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is interaction-based; no single-gene metabolic phenotype was shown.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to repression of PAP1/anthocyanin module through interaction with VAL1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect PAP1 repression inferred through VAL1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ4 physically associates with VAL1 in pull-down assays. 2. This interaction supports redundancy among JAZ repressors in stabilizing the VAL1-SNL-HDA19c repression module. 3. Direct metabolic phenotype for JAZ4 alone was not tested.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because direct interaction evidence is presented in Results.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to repression of PAP1/anthocyanin module through interaction with VAL1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect PAP1 repression inferred through VAL1 interaction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. JAZ9 C-terminal region interacts with VAL1 in yeast. 2. This supports JAZ family redundancy in linking JA signaling to the PAP1 repression module. 3. No standalone mutant phenotype was reported.
